revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Daibō Kofun]][[ja:大坊古墳 (玉名市)]]
{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan|qq=}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | name = Daibō Kofun | native_name =大坊古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Daibō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|32.948611|N|130.570556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Tamana, Kumamoto|en|Tamana, Kumamoto|qid=Q861610|lt=Tamana, Kumamoto|1=Tamana, Kumamoto}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]|qq=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|56|55|N|130|34|14|E|qq=}}|qq=}}|qq=}} The {{nihongo|'''Daibō Kofun'''|大坊古墳||qq=}} is a [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, located in the Tamana neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Tamana, Kumamoto|en|Tamana, Kumamoto|qid=Q861610|lt=Tamana, Kumamoto|1=Tamana, Kumamoto}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1977.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/192122|title=大坊古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021|qq=}}</ref> ==Overview== The Daibō Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the right bank of the Kikuchi River, which flows into the {{ill|Ariake Sea|en|Ariake Sea|qid=Q49557|lt=Ariake Sea|1=Ariake Sea}}, at the end of a hill that runs from east-to-west. It is orientated to the west, and has a multi-chamber horizontal-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] that opens from the center of the posterior circular rear portion to the south. The total length of the tomb is about 40 meters (estimated original length: 54 meters), with a 20-meter diameter circular portion, with a height of 4 meters. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1963, and the entire structure was revealed. The burial chamber is about six meters long; the inner chamber is 3.4 by 2.7 meters with a 2.8 height and the anterior chamber is 1.3 by 2.2 meters. The side walls are made of split {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|qid=Q181908|lt=Andesite|1=Andesite}} stone, and inside the inner chamber has an elaborate stone roof made of cut {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|qid=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}}. It is [[decorated kofun]] with triangular and circular patterns on both sides, the inner wall, and the front door stone of the inner chamber. The decorations on the pillars of the first and second gates of the stone chamber and the large stone sarcophagus-like structure in the inner chamber are done using vermilion and ultramarine, and the stone sarcophagus in particular has numerous continuous triangular patterns arranged in five tiers, with several circular patterns within. A large number of {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} were unearthed from the inner chamber, including straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron spears, {{ill|Celadon|en|Celadon|qid=Q1054559|lt=Celadon|1=Celadon}} bowls, gilt bronze rings, gold rings, and tubular beads, while apricot leaves, stirrups, knives, decorative metal fittings, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery were unearthed from the front chamber. Based on the variety of excavated items, the tumulus is believed to have been built in the mid-6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=}}{{in lang|ja|qq=}}</ref> The tomb is currently sealed off by a conservation facility, but is open to the public twice a year (October and March).. Approximately 500 meters to the northeast are the [[:en:Eianji Higashi Kofun|Eianji Higashi Kofun]] and [[:en:Eianji Nishi Kofun|Eianji Nishi Kofun]], which are also decorated kofun and separate National Historic Site.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The Daibō Kofun is about 850 meters northwest of {{ill|Shin-Tamana Station|en|Shin-Tamana Station|qid=Q1136695|lt=Shin-Tamana Station|1=Shin-Tamana Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Shinkansen|en|Kyushu Shinkansen|qid=Q11370254|lt=Kyushu Shinkansen|1=Kyushu Shinkansen}}. ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|qid=Q6573273|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)}} *[[Decorated kofun]] ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} ==External links== *[https://www.city.tamana.lg.jp/q/aview/331/163.html Tamana City home page] Daibō Kofun
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
[[en:Daibō Kofun]]
